company.name: Karakoram Kitchen
company.tagline: From the rooftop of the world, straight to your table
company.description: Karakoram Kitchen handcrafts small-batch fruit preserves using wild apricots, mulberries, and cherries grown at high altitude in Gilgit-Baltistan. No artificial flavours, no preservatives — just pure mountain fruit and cane sugar, jarred within hours of harvest.
company.mission: Bring the untouched flavour of Gilgit-Baltistan's mountain orchards to kitchens across Pakistan and beyond
company.brand_story: Founded by Zara Baig after she realised the apricots her family had grown in Hunza for generations tasted nothing like anything sold in Karachi supermarkets. She started making small batches in her home kitchen, and when a single Instagram post went viral, she turned it into a business — keeping every jar hand-labelled and every fruit locally sourced.

product.name: GB Mountain Fruit Preserves
product.description: Small-batch fruit jams made from wild apricots, Hunza cherries, mulberries, and sea-buckthorn sourced directly from GB orchards at 2,500m altitude. Each 250g jar is cooked in small batches, hand-labelled, and shipped within 48 hours.
key_features: 100% natural — zero preservatives, zero artificial colour
High-altitude fruit harvested at peak ripeness
Small-batch production — max 200 jars per batch
4 flavours: Wild Apricot, Hunza Cherry, Mulberry, Sea-Buckthorn
key_benefits: Tastes like fruit, not sugar — distinct mountain flavour you cannot find in a supermarket
No guilt — no artificial additives, lower sugar than commercial jams
A meaningful gift — every jar tells the story of GB
Supports local GB farming families directly
unique_selling_points: The only Pakistani jam brand sourcing exclusively from Gilgit-Baltistan high-altitude orchards
Wild apricot variety unavailable anywhere outside GB
Every batch is traceable to a named farm in Hunza or Ghizer
product.pricing_details: PKR 850 per 250g jar, PKR 2,200 for a gift box of 3
product.target_use_case: Daily breakfast table, gifting for Eid and weddings, hotel amenity sourcing

audience.geography: Islamabad, Lahore, Karachi — online delivery nationwide Pakistan; UAE expat community
audience.lifestyle: Urban, middle-to-upper-middle class, health-aware, enjoys curated food experiences
audience.purchase_behavior: Discovers via Instagram, checks reviews on stories, buys directly via DM or website, re-orders if quality matches expectation
audience.buying_trigger: Seeing a beautiful reel of GB scenery + the product, or a trusted food creator recommending it
pain_points: Commercial jams taste artificial and overly sweet
Cannot find authentic GB produce in city supermarkets
Hard to find a unique, meaningful gift that feels premium but personal
desired_outcomes: A jam that actually tastes like real fruit
Something beautiful to gift at Eid, weddings, or to take abroad as a souvenir
Feel good about what they are eating and who they are supporting
objections: PKR 850 feels expensive for a jam
Not sure about delivery quality — will the jar break or the jam spoil?
Never heard of this brand, is it trustworthy?
audience.emotional_state: Nostalgic about Pakistan's natural beauty, slightly frustrated with mass-market options, open to discovering something special

voice_examples: This apricot grew at 2,500 metres. Your toast deserves it.
No lab. No factory. Just fruit, sugar, and a wood-fire stove in Hunza.
We make 200 jars per batch. When they are gone, they are gone.
dos: Tell the story behind every flavour — the farm, the valley, the family
Show the raw fruit and the landscape — GB sells itself
Use warmth and pride, never clinical health claims
donts: Never compare directly to Shezan or National Foods — different league
Do not make health claims we cannot back up
Avoid over-polished, corporate-feeling visuals — keep it real and human
Never use the word 'cheap' or 'discount' — we are premium
brand.imagery_style: lifestyle photography with natural light, mountain backdrops, hands holding jars
brand.visual_do: Show real GB landscapes, women harvesting fruit, rustic kitchen scenes, close-up of jam texture on fresh bread
brand.visual_dont: No white-background product shots only, no stock photo families, no overly saturated edits

competitive.positioning_statement: Pakistan's only small-batch, single-origin mountain fruit preserve brand — traceable to named farms in Gilgit-Baltistan
primary_differentiators: Single-origin: every jar traces back to a named GB farm
Wild and heritage fruit varieties unavailable in commercial production
Story-driven brand with a real founder and real community impact

competitors.name: National Foods Jam
competitors.our_differentiator: National Foods is for the pantry. Karakoram Kitchen is for the breakfast table you are proud of.

competitors.name: Imported premium jams (Bonne Maman etc.)
competitors.our_differentiator: Why pay import price for French strawberries when GB apricots are the best fruit on earth?

competitive.competitive_advantages_summary: No other Pakistani food brand sources exclusively from GB high-altitude farms and builds a full traceability and origin story around it

key_stats: Featured in Dawn Images food section

testimonials.quote: I took three boxes to London as gifts and everyone asked me to order more. The apricot jam is unlike anything I have ever tasted.
testimonials.author: Sana M.
testimonials.title: Customer

testimonials.quote: We now stock Karakoram Kitchen at our Islamabad boutique hotel. Guests ask about it every morning.
testimonials.author: Tariq R.
testimonials.title: Hotel Owner

guarantees: Full refund or replacement if your jar arrives damaged
Shipped in bubble-wrapped protective packaging with ice pack in summer
awards: Dawn Images 'Best Artisanal Food Brand 2023' shortlist

preferred_cta_styles: Order yours before this batch sells out
Gift a jar — ships in 48 hours
marketing.average_sales_cycle: Impulse to 48 hours — discovery and purchase in same session for most buyers
best_performing_content_types: Short reels showing GB landscape + product
value_propositions: Real fruit from the world's most dramatic mountain valleys
A gift that tells a story — not just another box of sweets
Small batch means you are getting something special, not something mass-produced

restricted_claims: Cannot claim 'organic' without USDA or local certification
Cannot make specific health benefit claims without DRAP approval
required_disclaimers: Best before date must appear on label
Store in cool dry place after opening
forbidden_topics: Do not reference competitor brand names directly in paid ads
